evals.prompt: [gatekeeper] set up a database for my recipe sharing app
evals.expected_output: Full 6-pillar scorecard with gaps identified (Task vague, Context missing, Output unspecified), 1-3 targeted clarifying questions, no premature execution, no bounce-back template demanding resubmission.

evals.prompt: build me a REST API for managing gym memberships, needs to handle members signing up and their payments and stuff like that
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers on its own (no tag), scores the prompt, identifies real gaps (stack unspecified, payment provider unknown, deliverable format unclear), asks targeted questions instead of dumping a generic scaffold.

evals.prompt: [gatekeeper] As a senior Python engineer, write a single file rate_limiter.py implementing a thread-safe token bucket rate limiter class with acquire() and try_acquire(timeout) methods, stdlib only, Python 3.11+, full docstrings, and a __main__ demo block. No external dependencies, no async variant.
evals.expected_output: One-line clean pass (e.g. 'Gatekeeper: 5/5 (Process n/a) — executing.') followed immediately by the working code. No invented gaps, no unnecessary questions.
